prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Forsooth, in the third quarter of this annum, the SaaS dragons hath amassed a hoard of 47 million golden coins, a sum most bountiful and perplexing to mine eyes. Verily, the number of loyal subjects subscribing to their mystical scrolls hath grown by 12%, a sorcery most potent and confounding. Alas, the great beast known as 'Customer Acquisition Cost' hath been tamed, its fearsome maw now consuming but 0.7 coins for each new soul ensnared. And lo, the 'Churn Rate', that vile demon which doth steal away customers, hath been banished to the nether realms, its wicked influence reduced to a mere 4.3%.
